name: Adjustable Difficulty
description: A configurable difficulty layer that lets players adjust challenge pressure through explicit modifiers, presets, or assist settings without changing the core content structure.
design_notes: Adjustable difficulty should define difficulty_modifier_id records, setting scope, live-apply rules, save profile ownership, and which modes lock or disclose changes. Designers need to explain what each modifier changes, preserve player agency, and avoid hiding meaningful rules behind vague labels such as easy or hard.
design_purpose: Let players tune challenge pressure to match ability, fatigue, device, or learning needs.
Expose difficulty changes as understandable settings rather than hidden failure correction.
Support more play styles while preserving fixed-rule spaces such as ranked, challenge, or leaderboard modes.
player_fantasy: The player feels trusted to shape the challenge in a way that keeps the game playable and satisfying for them.

parameters.name: accessibility_profile_id
parameters.description: Profile record that owns the selected difficulty settings for this player.

parameters.name: difficulty_modifier_id
parameters.description: Named gameplay rule that can be adjusted through the difficulty menu.

parameters.name: modifier_step_count
parameters.description: Number of authored stops exposed for a specific modifier.

parameters.name: assist_intensity_level
parameters.description: Coarse preset tier used to apply several modifiers together.

parameters.name: setting_scope
parameters.description: Persistence boundary for the difficulty setting.

parameters.name: ranked_lock_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule for challenge, achievement, or leaderboard modes when modifiers are active.

edge_cases: A player changes enemy damage or timing assistance during a boss phase, checkpoint retry, or active multiplayer session.
A fixed-seed challenge or leaderboard run must decide whether modified settings invalidate ranking or use a separate board.
Cloud saves can merge difficulty settings from different devices with different platform defaults.
A content patch removes or renames difficulty_modifier_id records used by old saves.
common_bugs: The menu changes setting payloads but active enemies, timers, or encounters keep cached old modifier values.
Assist modifiers affect hidden achievement or leaderboard eligibility without player-facing disclosure.
Per-player local co-op settings overwrite each other because profile ids are stored globally.
Reset-to-default restores visual menu labels but not the underlying difficulty modifier records.
balancing_notes: Each modifier should describe the specific rule it changes so players can choose support without guessing.
Challenge and ranked modes should declare their allowed assist policy before the player invests effort.
Presets are useful onboarding, but granular settings help players solve one barrier without lowering everything.
accessibility_notes: Make difficulty options available before gameplay starts and from pause menus where technically safe.
Use plain-language descriptions and live previews for timing, damage, hint, and resource changes.

implementation_notes.unity: Store settings in an accessibility_profile_id asset or save record, then resolve difficulty_modifier_id values through a central service read by combat, puzzle, economy, and timer systems. UI Toolkit sliders should preview changes and commit through profile persistence with ranked_lock_policy_id disclosure.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep difficulty settings in a Resource or autoloaded settings service keyed by profile id. Gameplay nodes should query resolved modifiers rather than duplicating menu values, and save migration should map old modifier ids to current definitions.
implementation_notes.unreal: Represent modifiers as Data Assets, Gameplay Tags, or config rows applied through a player settings subsystem. Server or host authority should decide ranked locks and replicate only the resolved modifier set needed by gameplay.
implementation_notes.web: Store profile-scoped settings in account or local storage with a profile_save_version. Apply modifier payloads through pure lookup functions so Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom systems read the same committed difficulty state.

relationships.reason: Adjustable difficulty works best when assist features expose the specific timing, damage, or input modifiers being changed.
Tutorial and settings prompts can explain difficulty changes without forcing players to infer hidden rule changes.
Difficulty modifiers need policy rules for achievements or challenge rewards that depend on fixed constraints.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Includes Assist Mode options that alter challenge pressure while keeping play available.

example_games.title: The Last of Us Part II
example_games.note: Includes granular difficulty and accessibility settings across several systems.
